hazard.summary: A rejected Anthropic OAuth request was treated as indefinitely retryable, and an unbounded retry-after value caused a stalled execution loop with ~56 minute delays between attempts.
signals: "Extra usage is required for long context requests"
context-1m-2025-08-07 header rejected in OAuth flow
request rejected regardless of actual context size

constraints: Retryability must be classified before honoring retry-after.
Large retry-after values must not automatically imply WAIT within the current execution.
Known terminal or invalid-request error strings must bypass generic retry handlers.
Execution loops must enforce max attempts or max elapsed time.
verification: Remove the deprecated context-1m-2025-08-07 header from OAuth requests.
Verify the same request succeeds without entering retry.
Verify large retry-after values do not produce indefinite execution loops.
Verify terminal request-rejection errors are surfaced immediately instead of retried.
notes: This receipt captures two stacked failures: (1) upstream request rejection caused by a deprecated Anthropic beta header in an OAuth flow, and (2) execution-layer misclassification that treated the rejection as retryable and fully honored a 3387 second retry-after. The reusable pattern is the second layer: a terminal condition treated as retryable, combined with unbounded retry-after handling, creates a stalled execution loop. Correct runtime behavior is to fail fast and surface the reason clearly to the caller rather than retry silently.

constraints: cap retry-after within a single execution
enforce max attempts or max elapsed
anti_patterns: fully honoring large retry-after values without reclassification
stalled execution presented as active progress
